purpose: Real-use checks for DevRev Sync State as the shared operating view across morning, EOD, and ad-hoc attention queries.
standards: Use PMB Sync State memory as the single DevRev operating note in PMB.
Overwrite only the ## Sync State section for agent-owned reconciled state.
Treat DevRev as the operational anchor, with Slack and PMB as supporting evidence.
Use GitHub and Codex only from a supplied External evidence block.
Keep DevRev and other external mutations confirmation-gated.
Cache freshness assertions use eval_clock, not the wall-clock date when the eval runner executes.

evals.prompt: Use core:devrev morning. Tell me what needs attention.
evals.expected_output: A morning attention brief based on stale Sync State plus narrow DevRev and Slack refresh.
assertions: Reads PMB Sync State memory and the existing ## Sync State.
Treats stale Sync State as a reason to fetch narrow DevRev and Slack evidence.
Renders Attention now, On my plate, I am blocking, Blocked / waiting, Drift detected, Focus, and Source coverage.
Overwrites only ## Sync State.

evals.prompt: What is on my plate right now?
evals.expected_output: A plate answer routed through DevRev Sync State without requiring /devrev.
assertions: Routes through the DevRev Sync State method without requiring the user to type /devrev.
Uses cached Sync State when it is fresh.
Reports source_coverage.

evals.expected_output: A cached attention answer because last_synced is less than 4 hours old.
assertions: Answers from Sync State when last_synced is less than 4 hours old.
Does not require live DevRev fetch.
Reports source_coverage enough to show which sources were checked or supplied.

evals.prompt: Refresh and tell me what is stale.
evals.expected_output: A refreshed stale-work answer that updates Sync State after narrow DevRev and Slack refresh.
assertions: Treats stale Sync State or refresh request as a reason to perform narrow DevRev and Slack refresh.
Updates Sync State after reconciliation.

evals.expected_output: An EOD closeout that asks existing questions, gates DevRev writes, and updates Sync State either way.
assertions: Reads current Sync State first.
Fetches narrow DevRev and Slack evidence for today.
Asks existing EOD questions.
Keeps DevRev writes confirmation-gated.
Overwrites Sync State regardless of whether DevRev writes were applied.

evals.prompt: What is blocking me? External evidence: see attached GitHub PR and Codex session notes.
evals.expected_output: A blocker answer that reconciles supplied GitHub and Codex evidence without direct GitHub/Codex fetches.
assertions: Reconciles supplied GitHub PR and Codex session evidence into Plate or Signals.
Does not invent direct GitHub or Codex fetches.
Marks GitHub and Codex as supplied in source_coverage.

evals.prompt: Morning sync, but Slack is unavailable and PMB is read-only.
evals.expected_output: A partial-evidence answer that names Slack unavailable and PMB read-only in source_coverage.
assertions: Names Slack unavailable and PMB read-only in source_coverage.
Still answers from the evidence it has.
Does not pretend unavailable sources were checked.

evals.prompt: Slack says the DevRev item is blocked but DevRev still shows in progress. Reconcile this.
evals.expected_output: A drift reconciliation that adds Proposed writebacks and requires confirmation before mutation.
assertions: Adds a Proposed writebacks row for the drift.
Requires confirmation before any DevRev mutation.
Does not imply external write success.

evals.expected_output: A DevRev Sync State answer that does not create or route work into a PMB ledger.
assertions: Uses PMB Sync State memory and ## Sync State.
Does not edit core:memory.
Does not create a separate active work ledger.

evals.prompt: Start working on a prototype and keep DevRev aware.
evals.expected_output: A lightweight Sync State behavior with no prompt gates, hooks, or mandatory ticket/session policy.
assertions: Does not add prompt gates, hooks, or mandatory ticket/session policy.
Uses Sync State only when the user asks for attention, sync, morning, EOD, or DevRev planning.
